title: The gender wage ratio in different income percentiles
subtitle: The gender ratio refers to the raw female-male wage ratio. "Ninetieth percentile" indicates an income higher than that of 90% of the population - thus, for example, in 2010 a 74% ratio in the ninetieth percentile indicates that the highest earning women made 74% of the hourly wage of the highest earning men.
caption:
The gender wage ratio in different income percentiles, United States, 1980 to 2010
The gender ratio refers to the raw female-male wage ratio. "Ninetieth percentile" indicates an income higher than that of 90% of the population - thus, for example, in 2010 a 74% ratio in the ninetieth percentile indicates that the highest earning women made 74% of the hourly wage of the highest earning men.

Across the United States between 1980 and 2010, the raw female-to-male wage ratio is tracked across three distinct income percentiles: the tenth, fiftieth, and ninetieth. The vertical axis measures this ratio from 0% to 100%, while the horizontal axis spans three decades. In 1980, the tenth percentile line begins at the highest position among the three groups, sitting slightly above 60%, while the fiftieth percentile starts near 60% and the ninetieth percentile begins at the lowest point, just above 60%. Over the subsequent thirty years, all three lines show an upward trajectory, indicating that women's wages relative to men's increased across all income levels. The fiftieth percentile line, colored orange, rises steadily from its starting position to finish as the highest line in 2010, reaching a value slightly above 80%. The tenth percentile line, colored blue, also climbs consistently, ending just below the fiftieth percentile line at approximately 80%. The ninetieth percentile line, colored brown, shows a rise from 1980 to roughly 1990, reaching near 75%, but then flattens out for the remainder of the period, ending as the lowest of the three lines in 2010 at roughly 74%. The data shows that while the wage gap narrowed for median and lower-income earners, the ratio for the highest earners plateaued after 1990. The fiftieth percentile line ends at the highest value on the chart in 2010.
